title: Resume
text:
Download PDF
    
  

  Michael Lopez
  
    Data Strategist | Translating Complex Data into Community Action | Civic Tech & Nonprofit
  

  

  Experience

  
    Director, Data
    Child Poverty Action Lab · May 2023 - Present
    Leading data strategy, infrastructure, and team operations for a nonprofit focused on reducing child poverty in Dallas.
    
      Assumed data team management, vendor relationships, and stakeholder management responsibilities following CDO departure (Dec 2024 - Dec 2025), reporting to CTO
      Built project management system in Notion adopted org-wide by multiple departments
      Architecting migration to Databricks, systematizing 35-40 data pipelines to transition from file-based storage to scalable infrastructure
      Built eviction data pipeline processing 40,000+ Dallas County records annually, used by 12+ partners including Dallas Eviction Advocacy Center, Eviction Lab, and Dallas HHS
      Designed parcel-level housing tool with 20 active users identifying residents eligible for assistance programs
      Built data function from the ground up; led hiring for all data roles including organization's first data engineer
      Managed team of up to 4 (3 analysts + 1 engineer); created onboarding documentation and mentored team in R, SQL, and data visualization
      Evaluated and selected enterprise tools including Databricks, Claude Enterprise, Lucidchart, and data vendors (MySidewalk, DataAxle)
      Oversaw development of 30+ R Shiny dashboards informing decisions on housing stability, public safety, and resource allocation
    
  

  
    Manager, Data Analytics
    Child Poverty Action Lab · May 2022 - May 2023
    Transitioned from individual contributor to project leadership, managing cross-functional data initiatives and mentoring junior team members.
    
      Led development of R Shiny applications, shifting organization toward interactive data products
      Contributed to development of northtexasevictions.org, a public-facing eviction data transparency tool
      Mentored analytics interns; one intern subsequently hired as full-time analyst
      Managed projects spanning afterschool programming, child care accessibility, and housing stability
      Established data collection and sharing procedures that became organizational standards
    
  

  
    Associate, Analytics
    Child Poverty Action Lab · June 2020 - April 2022
    Early analytics team member who built foundational data infrastructure and reporting systems.
    
      Created initial eviction data pipeline in R, laying groundwork for system now processing 40K+ records annually
      Developed dashboards and reports in R, QGIS, Tableau, and ArcGIS for internal teams and community partners
      Automated routine data processes, establishing repeatable frameworks used across the organization
      Presented findings on housing instability, afterschool programming, and public safety to partner organizations
    
  

  
    Research Project Assistant
    UT Southwestern Medical Center · Dec 2019 - May 2020
    
      Conducted participant interviews and assessments for biomedical research projects
      Analyzed data and produced reports for internal research findings
      Performed literature and policy reviews; coordinated project logistics across departments
    
  

  
    Health and Social Policy Intern
    Children at Risk · June 2019 - August 2019
    
      Led policy research on early childhood development and school performance
      Developed policy briefs on Texas education best practices for senior leadership and state stakeholders
    
  

  
    Research Coordinator
    University of Texas at Dallas · Aug 2016 - Dec 2018
    
      Managed research operations for the Developmental Neurolinguistics Lab
      Recruited, trained, and supervised 10-15 research assistants each semester
      Recruited 300+ participants from local schools and youth programs
      Conducted community outreach, presenting findings to families and local organizations
    
  

  
    Research Assistant
    Florida International University · May 2013 - May 2015
    
      Assessed pre-kindergarten children's spatial abilities and verbal intelligence
      Trained research assistants on assessment protocols and transcription procedures
      Recruited participants across Miami-Dade County schools
    
  

  

  Education

  
    Master of Public Policy
    University of Texas at Dallas · 2021
  

  
    Bachelor of Arts, Psychology & Anthropology
    Florida International University · 2015
  

  

  Technical Skills

  
    Languages & Analytics: R, SQL, Python, Statistical Modeling, ETL
    Data Infrastructure: Databricks, CKAN, Jenkins, Git, CI/CD
    Visualization & Apps: R Shiny, Tableau, ggplot2, Quarto
    Geospatial: QGIS, ArcGIS, Leaflet, sf
    Platforms & Tools: Notion, Lucidchart, Claude Enterprise, PostgreSQL
    Domains: Housing, Public Safety, Social Policy, Community Development
  

  

  Selected Projects

  
    Dallas County Eviction Pipeline - Processing 40,000+ records annually, used by 12+ partners for outreach and intervention
    North Texas Evictions - Public-facing data transparency tool at northtexasevictions.org
    Parcel Block Walking Tool - Housing assistance eligibility identifier with 20 active users
    30+ R Shiny Dashboards - Interactive tools for housing stability, public safety, and resource allocation
    DigiLab TCG Standings - Shiny dashboard tracking Digimon TCG events in North Texas; built community for collaborative data collection (digilab.cards)
    atomtemplates - R package for standardized project creation and handling (GitHub)

title: About
text:
Hi, I'm Michael

  
    I build data systems that help communities. As Director of Data at Child Poverty Action Lab,
    I've spent nearly six years designing infrastructure that turns administrative data into
    action—from eviction records that connect families to legal aid, to tools that help
    organizations identify residents eligible for housing assistance.
  

  
    My background in psychology, anthropology, and public policy shaped how I approach this work.
    I'm most effective at the intersection of technical and programmatic—translating between
    technical teams and community stakeholders, ensuring analytical rigor while keeping the
    focus on real-world impact.
  

  What I've Built

  
    At CPAL, I've led the development of data infrastructure that serves real community needs:
  

  
    Data pipelines processing 40,000+ eviction records annually, used by partners including Dallas Eviction Advocacy Center and Eviction Lab
    30+ interactive dashboards (R Shiny) informing decisions on housing stability, public safety, and resource allocation
    Project management systems and operational processes now used org-wide
    Currently leading migration to Databricks, systematizing 35-40 data pipelines for long-term scale
  

  Technical Skills

  
    
      Languages & Analytics
      
        R (tidyverse, sf, ggplot2)
        SQL
        Python
        Statistical Modeling
      
    

    
      Data Infrastructure
      
        Databricks
        ETL / Data Pipelines
        Git / CI/CD
        CKAN / Jenkins
      
    

    
      Visualization & Apps
      
        R Shiny
        Tableau
        Quarto
        Leaflet / Mapbox
      
    

    
      Domain Expertise
      
        Housing & Eviction
        Public Safety
        Social Policy
        Community Development
      
    
  

  Let's Connect

  
    I'm always interested in connecting with others working at the intersection of data,
    technology, and social impact.
  

  
    lopezmikeok@gmail.com
    LinkedIn ·
    GitHub

    Data Strategist | Civic Tech & Social Impact
  
  
    
      
    
  





  Building Data Systems That Help Communities
  
    I design data infrastructure that turns administrative records into community action.
    With nearly six years in the nonprofit sector, I specialize in translating between
    technical teams and community stakeholders—building pipelines, dashboards, and tools
    that drive real-world impact in housing, public safety, and social policy.
